title: Eval Harness Setup
summary: Create the minimum evidence loop for prompts, routing, and retrieval changes before they reach production.
whenToUseIt: Use this when a team has prototypes or fragile manual QA but no stable release gate.
prerequisites: A draft quality bar for success vs failure

steps.title: Choose the critical user flows
steps.description: Select the narrow set of flows where regressions would actually damage trust, cost, or user value.

steps.title: Write the eval set
steps.description: Assemble real prompts, edge cases, and bad-case examples with expected outcomes and notes on failure modes.

steps.title: Define scoring
steps.description: Choose what counts as pass, warning, or failure for correctness, groundedness, format adherence, latency, and cost.
steps.aiPrompt: Turn this product quality bar into an eval scorecard with pass, warning, and fail thresholds.

steps.title: Automate execution
steps.description: Wire the eval set into CI or a repeatable local command so every significant change can be run the same way.

steps.title: Document release policy
steps.description: Record which metrics block release, which trigger manual review, and who approves exceptions.

expectedOutcome: A repeatable eval harness that catches regressions before users do.
commonMistakes: Writing synthetic evals that do not reflect real user behavior
Measuring only quality while ignoring latency or cost movement
Keeping eval logic in one engineer's head
